title: Immune system cells: innate immune response
body_html:
The following cells are mostly involved in the innate immune response:

Cell typeFunctions and properties
NeutrophilPrimary phagocytic cell in acute inflammation
Granules contain myeloperoxidase and lysozyme
Most common type of white blood cell
Multi-lobed nucleus
BasophilReleases histamine during allergic response
Granules contain histamine and heparin
Expresses IgE receptors on the cell surface
Bi-lobed nucleus
Mast cellPresent in tissues and are similar in function to basophils but derived from different cell lines
Releases histamine during allergic response
Granules contain histamine and heparin
Expresses IgE receptors on the cell surface
EosinophilDefends against protozoan and helminthic infections
Bi-lobed nucleus
MonocyteDiffferentiates into macrophages
Kidney shaped nucleus
MacrophageInvolved in phagocytosis of cellular debris and pathogens
Acts as an antigen presenting cell
Major source of IL-1
Natural killer cellInduce apoptosis in virally infected and tumour cells
Dendritic cellActs as an antigen presenting cell
4
200
Microbiology
